data.name: upward facing dog
instructions: Step:1 Lie face down on the floor with your legs extended behind you.
Step:2 Place your hands on the floor next to your lower ribs, fingers pointing forward.
Step:3 Press your hands firmly into the floor and straighten your arms, lifting your torso and thighs off the ground.
Step:4 Roll your shoulders back and down, opening your chest and lifting your gaze towards the ceiling.
Step:5 Hold this position for a few breaths, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: band underhand pulldown with classic
instructions: Step:1 Attach the band to a high anchor point, such as a pull-up bar or sturdy beam.
Step:2 Stand facing the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the band with an underhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Emphasize classic control.
Step:4 Extend your arms fully overhead, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Step:5 Engage your lats and pull the band down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: gentle style cable pulldown (pro lat bar)
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height so that your thighs are parallel to the ground and your feet are flat on the floor.
Step:2 Grasp the lat bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Sit down and lean back slightly, keeping your chest up and your back straight.
Step:4 Pull the bar down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Maintain gentle form throughout.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the bar back up to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: pull up (neutral grip)
instructions: Step:1 Hang from a pull-up bar with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and your arms fully extended.
Step:2 Engage your core and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Step:3 Pull your body up towards the bar by bending your elbows and driving your elbows down towards your hips.
Step:4 Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position with control.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: standing lateral stretch
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Step:2 Extend your arms straight out to the sides, parallel to the ground.
Step:3 Slowly lean your upper body to one side, feeling a stretch in your side and lats.
Step:4 Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
Step:5 Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: cable incline pushdown
instructions: Step:1 Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Step:2 Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Lean forward slightly and keep your back straight.
Step:5 Pull the bar down towards your thighs by extending your elbows.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: stability lever narrow grip seated row
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and footrests to ensure proper form.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with a narrow grip, palms facing each other.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward.
Step:5 Pull the handles towards your torso, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement.
Step:7 Slowly release the handles and return to the starting position. Focus on stability movement.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: extended style inverted row
instructions: Step:1 Set up a bar at waist height or use a suspension trainer.
Step:2 Stand facing the bar or suspension trainer, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Grab the bar or handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Lean back, keeping your body straight and your heels on the ground.
Step:5 Pull your chest towards the bar or handles, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Perform with extended intensity.

data.name: cable twisting pull with intensified
instructions: Step:1 Attach a cable handle to a low pulley and stand facing the machine.
Step:2 Grasp the handle with your left hand and step away from the machine, extending your arm fully.
Step:3 Position your feet shoulder-width apart, with your knees slightly bent.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Step:5 Pull the handle towards your body, rotating your torso to the right as you do so.
Step:6 Squeeze your back muscles at the end of the movement.
Step:7 Slowly return to the starting position, keeping tension on the cable.
Step:8 (intensified variation) Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and perform with your right hand.

data.name: bodyweight squatting row
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding onto a sturdy object or suspension trainer with your arms extended.
Step:2 Lower your body into a squat position, keeping your back straight and your knees behind your toes.
Step:3 From the squat position, pull your body up towards the object or suspension trainer, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: cable lateral pulldown with v-bar
instructions: Step:1 Sit down on the cable pulldown machine and grab the v-bar attachment with an overhand grip.
Step:2 Adjust the knee pad so that your thighs are secured under it.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and lean back slightly.
Step:4 Pull the v-bar down towards your upper chest while keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 Squeeze your back muscles at the bottom of the movement.
Step:6 Slowly return the v-bar to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: cable standing row (v-bar)
instructions: Step:1 Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Grasp the v-bar attachment with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step:3 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:4 Pull the v-bar towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: alternate lateral pulldown
instructions: Step:1 Sit on the cable machine with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Lean back slightly and pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: band fixed back close grip pulldown
instructions: Step:1 Attach the band to a fixed point above you, such as a pull-up bar or sturdy beam.
Step:2 Sit down on a bench or chair facing the band, with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Step:3 Grasp the band with a close grip, palms facing towards you.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Step:5 Pull the band down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, feeling the contraction in your lats.
Step:7 Slowly release the band back to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: suspended row
instructions: Step:1 Set up a suspension trainer at an appropriate height.
Step:2 Stand facing the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hold the handles with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Step:4 Lean back, keeping your body straight and your heels on the ground.
Step:5 Pull your chest towards the handles, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: lever pullover
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat and handles of the leverage machine to a comfortable position.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and grasp the handles with an overhand grip.
Step:3 Keep your arms slightly bent and your core engaged.
Step:4 Slowly pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your lats.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the peak contraction, then slowly return to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: assisted pull-up inclined
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the machine to your desired weight and height settings.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Hang with your arms fully extended and your feet off the ground.
Step:4 Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the handles, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Step:5 Continue pulling until your chin is above the handles.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Maintain inclined form throughout.

data.name: smith bent over row with declined
instructions: Step:1 Set up the smith machine with the bar at hip height.
Step:2 Stand facing the bar with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:5 Pull the bar towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Perform with declined intensity.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the bar back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: archer pull up
instructions: Step:1 Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:2 Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
Step:3 As you pull yourself up, bend one arm and bring your elbow towards your side, while keeping the other arm straight.
Step:4 Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar and your bent arm is fully flexed.
Step:5 Lower yourself back down with control, straightening the bent arm and repeating the movement on the other side.
Step:6 Alternate sides with each repetition.

data.name: lever front pulldown
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your knees under the pads and your feet flat on the ground.
Step:2 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Sit upright with your chest lifted and your shoulders back, maintaining a slight arch in your lower back.
Step:4 Engage your lats and pull the handles down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: lever seated row
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the seat height and footrests to a comfortable position.
Step:2 Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet on the footrests.
Step:3 Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, shoulder-width apart.
Step:4 Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Step:5 Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement.
Step:7 Slowly release the handles and return to the starting position.
Step:8 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: assisted standing chin-up
instructions: Step:1 Adjust the machine to your desired assistance level.
Step:2 Stand on the foot platform and grip the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step:3 Keep your chest up and shoulders back, engage your core, and slightly bend your knees.
Step:4 Pull your body up by flexing your elbows and driving your elbows down towards your sides.
Step:5 Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Step:6 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Step:7 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: dumbbell incline row
instructions: Step:1 Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Step:2 Grab a dumbbell in each hand and sit on the bench with your chest against the incline.
Step:3 Extend your arms fully, allowing the dumbbells to hang straight down from your shoulders.
Step:4 Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Step:5 Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Step:6 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: scapula dips
instructions: Step:1 Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended in front of you.
Step:2 Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Step:3 Lower your body by bending your elbows and retracting your shoulder blades, as if you are trying to squeeze a pencil between them.
Step:4 Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your hands to extend your elbows and return to the starting position.
Step:5 Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

data.name: kettlebell sumo high pull
instructions: Step:1 Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing outwards.
Step:2 Hold a kettlebell with both hands in front of your body, arms extended downwards.
Step:3 Bend your knees and lower your hips into a squat position, keeping your back straight.
Step:4 Drive through your heels and explosively extend your hips and knees, pulling the kettlebell up towards your chin.
Step:5 As you pull the kettlebell up, keep your elbows high and wide, and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Step:6 Lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
